title: Emotion Classifier Comparison Report

senticnet_word_level.description: Current SenticNet word-level emotion detection (13 cloud APIs)

A_hybrid_with_senticnet.approach: Hybrid: all-MiniLM-L6-v2 embeddings (384-dim) + 8 SenticNet features (392-dim) + LogisticRegression

C_distilbert_no_augment.note: Massively underfits with only 210 samples. 0% F1 on frustrated class.

C_distilbert_augmented.approach: Fine-tuned DistilBERT (distilbert-base-uncased) with HuggingFace emotion augmentation
C_distilbert_augmented.config: 10 epochs, lr=2e-5, augmented with dair-ai/emotion dataset

common_error_patterns: anxious ↔ overwhelmed confusion (3 errors in best model): Both involve stress/distress signals
frustrated ↔ overwhelmed confusion (2 errors): Both express negative high-arousal states
disengaged → joyful/overwhelmed (2 errors): Disengaged is semantically subtle, easy to confuse
focused → frustrated (1 error): 'locked into this problem' interpreted as frustration

reasons: Highest accuracy: 80% (vs 74% for A, 72% for C augmented)
Highest macro-F1: 0.803 (best balanced across all 6 classes)
Small model: ~80MB shared with existing embedding model
Works with small dataset: 210 samples are sufficient
No external data dependency: Doesn't need HuggingFace dataset augmentation
Reuses existing all-MiniLM-L6-v2 model (already loaded for activity classification)
recommendation.for_coaching_quality: Replacing current SenticNet word-level emotion detection (28%) with Approach B (80%) would provide 2.86x improvement in emotion accuracy. This directly impacts coaching quality since emotion-appropriate responses depend on correct emotion classification.
